title: Testing, Red Teaming, Release, Rollback, and Production Defense · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: An engineering, domain, security, privacy, accessibility, legal, finance, and operations board. The board needs defensible outcomes, source and relevance policy, component and end-to-end evaluations, security and privacy red teams, accessible evidence, capacity and total cost, immutable versions, canary migration, deletion, rollback, restore, incident drills, ownership, and residual-risk acceptance. Planned artifact: Deliver Enterprise RAG Production Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric def…
objectives: Defend stakeholder outcome, sources, relevance, retrieval, generation, citations, safety, privacy, accessibility, reliability, capacity, cost, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.
Promote immutable corpus, index, model, prompt, policy, and application versions through shadow, canary, slice, quality, safety, latency, cost, abort, and rollback gates.
Red team poisoning, indirect injection, embedding manipulation, tenant leaks, secret extraction, unsafe output, tool effects, denial of service, deletion, and incident response.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Rag Production Defense
steps.instruction:
project case — A project RAG engineering team handles case 7344 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate release on component, end-to-end, security, accessibility, load, rollback, and recovery evidence; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Rag Incident Causal Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-incident-causal-recovery-x6ac8a" that uses case 1956 to trace every RAG stage with bounded redacted signals, SLOs, causal faults, and rollback readiness. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Rag Incident Causal Recovery.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Incident Causal Recovery into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Rag Incident Causal Recovery, keep Rag Incident Causal Recovery connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-incident-causal-recovery-x6ac8a
# Competency: rag-incident-causal-recovery.
# Case 1956: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
def evidence_rag_incident_causal_recovery_x6ac8a(trace_stages: int = 6, expected_stages: int = 6, retrieval_p95_ms: float = 42.0, error_rate: float = 0.01, content_logged: bool = False, rollback_ready: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_x6ac8a = "x6ac8a-a8ca6x-1956"
    assert evidence_variant_x6ac8a
    evidence_axes_x6ac8a = (
        "corpusx6ac8a",
        "querya8ca6x",
        "tenant1956",
        "indexx6ac8a1956",
        "prompta8ca6x1956",
        "evaluation1956x6ac8a",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_x6ac8a)) == 6
    if trace_stages != expected_stages or expected_stages < 5:
        return False, trace_stages, "trace-completeness"
    if retrieval_p95_ms <= 0.0 or error_rate < 0.0 or error_rate > 1.0:
        return False, error_rate, "service-signal"
    if content_logged:
        return False, error_rate, "sensitive-telemetry"
    if not rollback_ready:
        return False, error_rate, "recovery-readiness"
    assert trace_stages == expected_stages
    return True, round(retrieval_p95_ms, 2), "redacted-stage-trace"
